### Setup

```{r compare, echo=FALSE, eval=TRUE}
#png("precomputed/vg2_TFP_stan_comp.png")
par(mfrow=c(1,2))
#plot(density(extract(stan_fit_2,"mu0")$mu0),main="mu0")
#plot(density(extract(stan_fit_2,"beta[1,1]")$"beta[1,1]"),main="beta[1,1]")
#lines(density(c(py$samplesflat[[1]])),col="red")
#dev.off()
#print(summary(stan_fit_2))

# Create a single ggplot with density and Vegas probability line/points combined
gg_combined <- ggplot(data.frame(
  x = c(extract(stan_fit_2, "beta[1,1"])$beta, py$a0np),
  y = c(density(extract(stan_fit_2, "beta[1,1"])$beta, n = 100)$y, py$prob_dens)
), aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_density(fill = "blue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_line(color = "red") +
  geom_point(color = "brown", shape = 21, fill = "brown") +
  labs(title = "Density Plot of beta[1,1] with Vegas Comparison", x = "beta[1,1]/a0", y = "Density/Probability")

# Remove the par(mfrow) and png calls since we're making a single plot
grid.arrange(gg_combined, ncol = 1)
